issuesFound.explanation: The small button has a min-height of 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor disabilities or using touch screens may struggle to activate the control accurately. This violates WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px, min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons have a 20px×20px minimum size, below the 24px minimum. Touch screen users and users with fine motor control issues may accidentally activate adjacent elements. This fails WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 550: min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px, height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28px×28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size. While it meets the 24px absolute minimum, it may still be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate reliably.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width 20px, height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are 20px×20px, below the 24px minimum touch target. Users with dexterity impairments may miss the control when tapping, causing form errors. This violates WCAG 2.5.8.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: width: 24px; height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a minimum height of 24px without sufficient padding, potentially falling below the 44px recommended touch target. Users with motor disabilities may struggle to tap these links accurately on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3);

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, making content visible on screen. Screen reader users may encounter duplicate content, while sighted users see hidden text, causing confusion and violating WCAG 1.3.2 Meaningful Sequence.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280: position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0;

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body word-spacing -0.2em
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, compressing text and reducing readability.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_onToggleClick` in the pricing module toggles the billing period but does not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger element. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded/collapsed state of the controlled content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2070: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-expanded', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');` after `_onToggleClick` function definition.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function `setPeriod` changes visible pricing content but does not announce the change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users will not be notified of the updated prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2080: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Billing period changed to ' + period);` after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_validateFile` in the file upload module validates file size and type but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid input element. Screen reader users will not be alerted to the validation error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2086: Add `inputElement.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after the validation error is determined (where `inputElement` is the file input).

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have `aria-expanded="false"` set initially when the menu is closed. Screen reader users will not know the initial state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2090: In `mobileNav.init`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_trigger` is assigned.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_closeInstance` in the dropdown menu module hides the panel by setting `hidden` attribute but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. When `hidden` is not supported, the element remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2094: Add `inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_onPrev` in the carousel module changes the visible slide but does not announce the change via an aria-live region. Screen reader users will not be notified of the new slide.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2098: Add `liveRegion.announce('Slide ' + (_current + 1) + ' of ' + _slides.length);` after `goTo(_current - 1, 'backward');`.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The element with `data-billing-toggle` has a click event listener but no keydown handler for Enter and Space. Keyboard users cannot activate the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2102: Add `_toggleEl.addEventListener('keydown', _onToggleKeydown);` after the click event listener in `pricing.init`.

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open, close, and toggle functions change the visible state of the drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know whether the navigation is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In the open function, after setting _open = true, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); In the close function, after setting _open = false, add: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); In the toggle function, update aria-expanded based on the new state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide the panel but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the dropdown's state.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, after inst.open = true, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); In _closeInstance, after inst.open = false, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button cycles between monthly and annual states but does not set aria-pressed to reflect the pressed state. Screen reader users cannot perceive which option is selected.
issuesFound.fix: In the _onToggleClick function, after determining the next period, update aria-pressed on _toggleEl. For example: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, next === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates visibly but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the result count change.
issuesFound.fix: In the applyFilters function, after updating the count badge, add a live region announcement. For example: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: pricing dynamic updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices visibly but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the change.
issuesFound.fix: In the setPeriod function, after updating the prices, add a live region announcement. For example: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true". Screen reader users may not be alerted to the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function, in the else block (when result.valid is false), add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the inlineValidator removes error styling but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function, in the if block (when result.valid is true), add: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialise aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set initially. Screen reader users cannot know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the init function of mobileNav, after obtaining _trigger, set its initial aria-expanded to false: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialise aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded set initially. Screen reader users cannot know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the register function of dropdownMenu, after setting aria-haspopup and aria-controls, set aria-expanded to false: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-controls pointing to the drawer element. Screen reader users cannot understand which element is controlled.
issuesFound.fix: In the init function of mobileNav, after obtaining _trigger and _drawer, set aria-controls on _trigger: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not set aria-hidden when hiding drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it is hidden visually but aria-hidden is not set. Screen reader users may still encounter the hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: In the close function of mobileNav, after removing the open class, add: setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true'); In the open function, remove aria-hidden: removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not set aria-hidden when hiding panel
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, it is hidden visually but aria-hidden is not set. Screen reader users may still encounter the hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: In _closeInstance, after adding the hidden attribute, add: setAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true'); In _openInstance, after removing hidden, add: removeAttr(inst.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion does not manage aria-hidden on panels
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ answer is collapsed, aria-hidden is not set on the panel; when expanded, aria-hidden is not removed. Screen reader users may incorrectly hear hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: In the open function, after removing the hidden attribute, add: removeAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN); In the close function, after setting hidden, add: setAttr(item.panel, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus are not announced to screen reader users. They may not know that a menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, after opening the panel, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Dropdown opened'); In _closeInstance, after closing the panel, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Dropdown closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer are not announced to screen reader users. They may not know that the navigation menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: In the open function, after adding the open class, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened'); In the close function, after removing the open class, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: productFilter does not announce filter changes

issuesFound.title: Required field marker asterisk not hidden from screen readers

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy when loading

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified when the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1166: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function sets up event listeners for the navigation toggle but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state. This leaves the trigger without a state indicator until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1218: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function removes the visible class from the overlay and eventually sets the `hidden` attribute, but it does not explicitly set `aria-hidden="true"`. This can lead to inconsistent behavior in some assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1168: `_overlay.setAttribute(ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the page structure significantly, but there is no dedicated `liveRegion.announce` call within these functions to notify the user of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`, Line 1166: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.togglePlay` function switches the carousel between playing and paused states, but it does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the `_playBtn`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1888: `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, _playing ? 'true' : 'false');`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds a CSS class `CSS.fieldInvalid` and updates the error message, but it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2188: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, `inlineValidator._renderResult` removes the error class but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2183: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function sets up the trigger and drawer but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state. This leaves the trigger's state ambiguous to assistive technology until the first interaction.

issuesFound.title: user action result not announced via aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `scrollToTop._onClick` function scrolls the page to the top and moves focus, but it does not provide a live-region announcement. Users may be confused by the sudden jump in page position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1368: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop);` inside the `_onClick` handler.

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `testimonialsCarousel.togglePlay` function cycles the auto-play state, but the `_playBtn` does not have its `aria-pressed` attribute updated in a way that reflects the current state of the toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1848: update `togglePlay` to call `play()` or `pause()`, and ensure `play()` sets `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true');` and `pause()` sets `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds a CSS class `CSS.fieldInvalid` and updates the error element, but it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field itself.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2148: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside the `else` block of `_renderResult`.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function removes the invalid CSS class when a field becomes valid, but it does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2144: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` inside the `if (result.valid)` block of `_renderResult`.

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function removes the `CSS.visible` class from the overlay and eventually sets the `hidden` attribute, but it does not explicitly set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer or overlay during the transition.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1063: add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` and `setAttr(_overlay, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true');` inside `close`.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `:focus-visible` removes the native outline (`outline: none`) but does not provide any visible replacement (no non‑zero outline, border, or box‑shadow). Keyboard users lose the focus ring, making navigation impossible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` removes the outline (`outline: none`) without supplying an alternative visual cue, causing focus loss for keyboard users who navigate with non‑mouse input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 383: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` with only an offset, providing no visible focus indicator for button elements, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible` removes the outline (`outline: none`) without any replacement, leaving the mobile navigation toggle unfocusable for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 511: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 620: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 735: `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: `.footer-brand a:focus-visible` removes the outline and only adds a border‑radius, leaving footer brand links without a visible focus cue.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1062: `.footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links lose their outline (`outline: none`) with no replacement, breaking focus visibility for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1070: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social‑media link anchors remove the outline without any alternative visual indicator, making them invisible to keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove the outline (`outline: none`) and set a negative offset, providing no visible focus style for users navigating via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1155: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 2px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm (min-height: 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` class defines a minimum height of 20 px, which is smaller than the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24 px, making small buttons hard to tap for touch users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 492: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon (min-width/min-height: 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` sets both `min-width` and `min-height` to 20 px, below the required 24 px touch target size, reducing tap accuracy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 511: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers (.sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `clip: auto`, which does **not** hide content visually while keeping it accessible. This defeats its purpose and may expose hidden content to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace the block with a correct implementation, e.g.:  
  Line 409: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0 0 0 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates `transform` (spatial movement) without a corresponding `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who reduce motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an override inside the existing reduced‑motion block:  
  Line 822 (within `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`): `.hero-image img { transition: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of `.hero-image img` applies a `transform` animation without a reduced‑motion override, causing unwanted motion for motion‑sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside the reduced‑motion block add:  
  Line 828: `.hero-image img:hover { transform: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` includes a `transform` transition (used for active state) without a reduced‑motion override, potentially causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Within the reduced‑motion block add:  
  Line 447: `.btn { transition: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of `.hero-ctas .btn-primary` applies `transform: translateY(-1px)` with a transition, but no reduced‑motion override is provided.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside the reduced‑motion block add:  
  Line 896: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability on body (word-spacing: -0.2em)
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which compresses word gaps and harms readability for all users, especially those with cognitive or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 226: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative (a { text-decoration: none; })
issuesFound.explanation: The generic `a` selector removes underlines (`text-decoration: none`) and no other non‑colour visual differentiator (e.g., border‑bottom, background change) is provided for body‑text links, making links indistinguishable for users who cannot rely on colour alone.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a visual cue, e.g.:  
  Line 254: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` (or add a border‑bottom on hover)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The generic `:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none` and only an offset, removing the default focus ring for all focusable elements without providing any visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: This rule removes the outline for elements that receive focus via mouse or touch (`outline: none`) and does not supply an alternative visual cue, leaving keyboard‑only users without a focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 306: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.explanation: Sub‑menu links remove the outline (`outline: none`) and only offset it, providing no visible focus cue for keyboard users navigating the dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 822: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 1060: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove their focus outline (`outline: none`) without providing any other visual focus style, making them invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: `.footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in the footer strip the focus outline (`outline: none`) and do not replace it, breaking focus visibility in the site’s primary navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1390: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social‑media link icons remove the focus outline (`outline: none`) without any substitute, preventing keyboard users from locating these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch‑target size of 44 × 44 px, making it difficult for finger users to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; /* increased to meet minimum target size */ }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only buttons define `min-width: 20px` and `min-height: 20px`, both under the required 44 px minimum, creating a small tap area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 740: `.btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers (.sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static; width: auto; height: auto; clip: auto;` etc., which does **not** hide the element visually while keeping it accessible. Instead it leaves the element in the normal flow, defeating its purpose and potentially exposing decorative content to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` rule animates `transform` on hover, creating spatial movement, but there is no corresponding `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override that disables or reduces the animation for users who request reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1320: `.hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); }`  
  Line 1330 (inside existing reduced‑motion block): `.hero-image img { transition: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of the primary CTA button applies `transform: translateY(-1px)`, a spatial movement, without a reduced‑motion media query to suppress it for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1350: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); }`  
  Line 1360 (inside the existing reduced‑motion block): `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which compresses the space between words and can make text difficult to read, especially for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 218: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes the default underline from all links, and no other visual differentiator (such as color change, border, or background) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 246: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`   (or add a distinct style, e.g., `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;`)

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update `aria‑expanded` on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) is used to open/close the off‑canvas navigation, but the code never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` or `"false"` on that button. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling the drawer, add `_trigger.setAttribute('aria‑expanded', _open ? 'true' : 'false');` in both `open()` and `close()`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria‑controls` reference to the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The button that controls the navigation drawer has no `aria‑controls` pointing to the drawer’s `id`. Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it shows/hides.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer element has an `id` (e.g., `nav-drawer`) and set `_trigger.setAttribute('aria‑controls', 'nav-drawer');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not update `aria‑expanded` on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed the script only adds/removes CSS classes and the `hidden` attribute on the panel. It never updates the `aria‑expanded` state on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance()` add `trigger.setAttribute('aria‑expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance()` add `trigger.setAttribute('aria‑expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing `aria‑controls` to its panel
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button receives `aria‑haspopup="true"` but no `aria‑controls` attribute linking it to the panel, so assistive tech cannot locate the controlled element.
issuesFound.fix:
After generating a unique `id` for the panel, set `trigger.setAttribute('aria‑controls', panelId);`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not update `aria‑expanded` on its question button
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ items use `aria‑expanded` only on initial load; subsequent toggles never modify this attribute, so the expanded/collapsed state is not communicated to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open(item)` add `item.trigger.setAttribute('aria‑expanded', 'true');` and in `close(item)` add `item.trigger.setAttribute('aria‑expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ triggers missing `aria‑controls` to their answer panels
issuesFound.explanation: The script generates `id`s for the trigger and panel but does not set `aria‑controls` on the trigger, breaking the relationship for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating `panelId`, call `trigger.setAttribute('aria‑controls', panelId);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle (monthly/annual) does not use `aria‑pressed` or `aria‑expanded` to convey state
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off switch toggles the billing period but only adds a CSS class; it never updates `aria‑pressed` or `aria‑expanded`, leaving the current period ambiguous to AT users.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, set `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria‑pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing `aria‑controls` to the pricing cards it affects
issuesFound.explanation: The button that switches billing periods does not reference the pricing cards it updates, so AT cannot infer which region is being affected.
issuesFound.fix:
Give the pricing container an `id` (e.g., `pricing-cards`) and set `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria‑controls', 'pricing-cards');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, the visible count is updated, but no `aria‑live` region is used, so users of assistive tech are unaware of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden `
` and update its `textContent` with the count each time the filter runs. ---

issuesFound.title: Inline field validation does not set `aria‑invalid` on invalid inputs
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the script adds error text but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the input, violating ARIA best practice and preventing AT from announcing the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In the `!result.valid` branch, call `field.setAttribute('aria‑invalid', 'true');` and remove it when the field becomes valid.

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation error messages are not linked to the inputs via `aria‑describedby` (and the inputs lack `aria‑invalid`)
issuesFound.explanation: The helper only adds the error element to `aria‑describedby` but never sets `aria‑invalid` on the field, so screen readers do not convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `markInvalid()` add `field.setAttribute('aria‑invalid', 'true');` and in `markValid()` remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden navigation drawer does not receive `aria‑hidden` when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by removing CSS classes, but the element’s `aria‑hidden` state is never set, so AT may still read its content.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()` add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria‑hidden', 'true');` and in `open()` remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden with the `hidden` attribute but not given `aria‑hidden` when closed, and not cleared when opened
issuesFound.explanation: The panel’s visibility is toggled via the `hidden` attribute only; AT may still expose the content.
issuesFound.fix:
When closing, add `panel.setAttribute('aria‑hidden', 'true');` and when opening, remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: User actions that change the product‑filter view mode (grid ↔ list) are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching the view mode updates the UI but provides no `aria‑live` announcement, leaving AT users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling, update a hidden live‑region element (e.g., `#filter‑status`) with “List view enabled” or “Grid view enabled”.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new period via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Changing from monthly to annual billing updates the UI but does not inform screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, set `liveRegion.announce('Billing period switched to annual');` or similar.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close functions do not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.open()` toggles CSS classes and focus but never updates `aria-expanded` on the trigger button, leaving the ARIA state out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add the attribute inside `open()` and `close()`:  
```javascript
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');   // in open()
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // in close()
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden without removing it from the accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed the code adds a CSS class and removes it from view but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element, so screen readers may still read its content.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
if (_drawer) _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons do not update `aria-expanded` when the menu opens or closes
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a dropdown receives a visual class change but never receives an updated `aria-expanded` value, breaking the required ARIA state for disclosure widgets.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');   // when opening
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');  // when closing
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden without setting `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden by adding a CSS class and the `hidden` attribute, but `aria-hidden="true"` is never applied, so the panel remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close actions are not announced to screen‑reader users
issuesFound.explanation: No live‑region announcement is made when a dropdown menu is opened or closed, leaving users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing the state, add:  
```javascript
liveRegion.announce(inst.open ? 'Menu opened' : 'Menu closed');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update `aria-expanded` when a question is expanded or collapsed
issuesFound.explanation: The `<button>` that toggles an FAQ answer never receives an updated `aria-expanded` value, violating the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');   // on open
setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');  // on close
```  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ answer panels are hidden without `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: Panels are hidden via `hidden` attribute only; `aria-hidden="true"` is not set, so hidden content stays in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: No `liveRegion.announce` call is made when an FAQ item is opened or closed, so users of assistive technology receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
Add after state change:  
```javascript
liveRegion.announce(item.open ? `${item.question} – answer expanded` : `${item.question} – answer collapsed`);
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search input does not set `aria-expanded` when the suggestion list opens
issuesFound.explanation: The `<input>` that controls the autocomplete list never receives `aria-expanded="true"` when the list becomes visible, breaking the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');   // when opening
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); // when closing
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Autocomplete suggestion list is hidden without `aria-hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: The listbox is hidden by adding the `hidden` attribute only; `aria-hidden="true"` is not applied, leaving the list in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set `aria-invalid` on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the required ARIA state `aria-invalid="true"` is never set, so assistive technologies cannot convey the error.
issuesFound.fix:
In `inlineValidator.validate` replace the call to `srUtils.markInvalid` with:  
```javascript
field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear `aria-invalid` on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: Once a field has been marked invalid, the `aria-invalid` attribute remains even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix:
When the field is valid, remove the attribute:  
```javascript
field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update `aria-pressed` when the drawer opens/closes
issuesFound.explanation: The button acts as a binary toggle but never receives `aria-pressed` updates, violating the toggle button pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true');   // in open()
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false');  // in close()
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons do not update `aria-pressed` when the menu is opened/closed
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger is a button that toggles a menu but never receives `aria-pressed` updates.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true');   // when opening
setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false'); // when closing
```  

These issues cover all mandatory JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) identified in the provided script.

fpTitles: Dropdown panels are hidden without setting `aria-hidden`
FAQ answer panels are hidden without `aria-hidden`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing `aria-controls` on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav “hamburger” button (`SELECTORS.navToggle`) is used to open/close the navigation drawer via `setExpanded(trigger, true/false)`, but the script never adds an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the drawer element’s `id`. Assistive technologies therefore cannot associate the button with the region it controls.
issuesFound.fix:
After the drawer element is identified, add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-controls', drawer.id);` (or generate an `id` for the drawer if missing) during initialization.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open handler does not update `aria‑expanded` on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `dropdownMenu._openInstance()` adds the CSS class `open` to the panel and removes `hidden`, but it never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to reflect the open state. Users of assistive technology cannot know whether the dropdown is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `_openInstance()`, after opening the panel, add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`. Likewise, `_closeInstance()` should set it to `'false'`.

---

issuesFound.title: Site‑search suggestion list open does not set `aria‑expanded` on the input
issuesFound.explanation: When the search input gains focus or the user types, the suggestion panel is shown (`_open()`), but the input element never receives an `aria-expanded` attribute indicating the listbox’s visibility. This breaks the combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
In `siteSearch._open()` (or after `suggestionListRenderer.show()`), add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. When closing, set it to `'false'`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close does not update `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open(item)` and `close(item)` animate the panel but never toggle `aria-expanded` on the question button (`item.trigger`). The ARIA state therefore does not reflect the visible state of the answer.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open(item)`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. In `close(item)`, set it to `'false'`.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator never sets `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` calls `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)`, which only adds `aria-describedby` linking to the error message. It does **not** set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field, nor does it remove it on valid input. This omission prevents screen readers from announcing the field’s error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_renderResult()`, when `result.valid` is false, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`. When valid, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button (monthly/annual switch) does not set `aria‑expanded` or `aria‑controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The simple on/off toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) changes visual state but never indicates via ARIA whether the associated pricing table is expanded or collapsed, nor does it reference the table with `aria-controls`.
issuesFound.fix:
Assign an `id` to the pricing comparison table, then in `_onToggleClick()` set `trigger.setAttribute('aria-controls', tableId);` and toggle `aria-expanded` between `"true"` and `"false"`.

---

issuesFound.title: Multi‑step wizard navigation buttons lack `aria‑controls` linking to their panels
issuesFound.explanation: The “Previous”, “Next”, and “Submit” buttons control which wizard step panel is shown, but they do not have `aria-controls` attributes pointing to the corresponding step containers. This prevents assistive technologies from exposing the relationship.
issuesFound.fix:
During `multiStepWizard.init()`, assign each step panel an `id` (if missing) and set `prevBtn.setAttribute('aria-controls', currentPanel.id);` etc., updating the attribute each time the step changes.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close handler does not reset `aria‑expanded` on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance()` hides the panel and removes the open class, but it never sets `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button, leaving the ARIA state stale after the menu is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');` inside `_closeInstance()` after the panel is hidden.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow in the same rule-block. outline-offset alone does not render a visible ring. This removes keyboard focus indication for all elements, failing WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode

issuesFound.fix: Line 345: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which does not hide content visually. Content intended for screen readers only will be visible to all users, causing clutter and confusion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform and .btn:active uses transform: translateY. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block disabling this motion for .btn, which can cause discomfort for vestibular disorder users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 513: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation override is empty: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module toggles the visibility of the navigation drawer (_drawer) and overlay (_overlay) but fails to update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users are not informed whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line mobileNav.close: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module shows and hides the panel element (inst.panel) using the hidden attribute but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technology from conveying the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function reveals the answer panel (item.panel) but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons ([data-period]) toggle their active visual state but do not update the aria-pressed attribute. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing._syncToggleState: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (_toggleEl) is assigned role="switch" but its state (aria-checked) is never updated when clicked or synced. The visual class changes, but the accessibility state remains static.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing._syncToggleState: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(isYear));

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description reference but does not remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute on the field. If a field was previously invalid, it remains marked as invalid to assistive technology even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count badge lacks aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: The product count badge (_countBadge) is updated dynamically when filters are applied, but the element does not have an aria-live attribute. Screen reader users are not announced the new count of visible products.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.init: setAttr(_countBadge, ARIA.LIVE, 'polite');

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button (_btn) is shown and hidden by toggling the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but the aria-hidden attribute is not updated. The button may remain in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line scrollToTop._update (show): _btn.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line scrollToTop._update (hide): _btn.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu panel not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel (inst.panel) is hidden using the hidden attribute but aria-hidden is not toggled. When closed, the panel content may still be accessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: inst.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: inst.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch listbox not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion listbox (_listEl) is hidden using the hidden attribute but aria-hidden is not toggled. When closed, the suggestions may still be accessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line suggestionListRenderer.hide: _listEl.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  Line suggestionListRenderer.show: _listEl.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer (_drawer) visibility is toggled via CSS classes and the overlay uses hidden, but the drawer itself does not have aria-hidden toggled. When closed, the nav content may remain accessible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open: _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line mobileNav.close: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes (e.g., monthly to annual), prices are updated dynamically but no announcement is made via a live region. Users are not informed that the prices have switched.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button moves the viewport but does not announce the action via a live region. Screen reader users may be disoriented by the sudden change in position.
issuesFound.fix: Line scrollToTop._onClick: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds an error description but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, preventing users from understanding the error state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description but does not remove or reset aria-invalid. Fields remain marked as invalid to assistive technology even after correction.

issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function toggles the drawer visibility but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed that the menu is now open.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger aria-expanded not updated on close
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function hides the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed that the menu is now closed.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger aria-expanded not initialised on load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function attaches event listeners but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger. The button's state is undefined until the first interaction.

issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function sets hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer. The content may still be accessible to screen readers despite being visually hidden.

issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function removes hidden attribute but does not remove aria-hidden from the drawer. The content may remain hidden from screen readers despite being visible.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions change visible content but do not write to a live region. Users are not notified of the menu state change.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger aria-expanded not updated on open
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function shows the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. Screen reader users are not informed the dropdown is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger aria-expanded not updated on close

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger aria-expanded not initialised on load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-haspopup but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state. The trigger's state is undefined until interaction.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function sets hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the panel. Content may remain accessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel not added to accessibility tree when open
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes hidden attribute but does not remove aria-hidden from the panel. Content may remain hidden from screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates visible prices but does not announce the change via a live region. Users are not notified of the new billing period.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger aria-expanded not updated on open
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. Screen reader users are not informed the answer is visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel not removed from accessibility tree when closed

issuesFound.title: Search input aria-expanded not updated on close

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 573: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. This creates a too-small touch target for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 585: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Form checkboxes have width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes them difficult to tap accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1516: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class sets min-height: 32px. While this meets the 24px minimum, it is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets, potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments unless additional spacing is provided.
issuesFound.fix: Line 497: `.btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn-lg
issuesFound.explanation: The large button variant sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 579: `.btn-lg { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-8); font-size: var(--font-size-lg); border-radius: var(--radius-xl); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle has min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 631: `.nav-toggle { display: none; min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on navigation menu items
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links and buttons in #nav-menu have min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 657: `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-1); padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-700); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-md); border: none; background: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links have min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 696: `[id$="-submenu"] li a { display: block; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 44px; display: flex; align-items: center; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has height: 28px, below the 44px recommended touch target height.
issuesFound.fix: Line 724: `#search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); padding-right: var(--space-10); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-50); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-full); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width: 28px and height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 733: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on filter tab buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons have min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1093: `.filter-tabs button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1215: `.pricing-toggle button { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-5); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px, which meets the minimum but is below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1549: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on social media links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1561: `.social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause motion animation. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for this selector to disable the transform transition for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 506: (add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block or create new) Line 891: `.btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform property. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 891 contains empty rules for this selector, which does not actually disable the transition or transform.
issuesFound.fix: Line 891: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, a negative value that reduces space between words. This can impair readability for users with dyslexia or low vision by making text harder to parse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 275: `body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links (nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a) have text-decoration: none with no non-colour visual differentiator on hover (only color changes). Users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 761: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none with no non-colour visual differentiator on hover (only color changes). This relies solely on colour to identify links, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1553: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: Section header paragraphs use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.8:1. This fails the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text (12px-16px) under WCAG 1.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1052: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);` (or darker colour meeting 4.5:1)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hints
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints use --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on white background, giving approximately 2.8:1 contrast. At font-size-xs (approximately 9px), this small text fails the 4.5:1 contrast requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1524: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Primary buttons use white text (#ffffff) on --colour-brand-primary (#5588bb), resulting in approximately 3.9:1 contrast. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (12px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 500: `color: var(--colour-neutral-900);` (or darken background to #004aaa which has 7.5:1)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on outline buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Outline buttons use --colour-brand-primary (#5588bb) text on white background, resulting in approximately 3.9:1 contrast. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (12px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);` (or #004aaa)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on ghost buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Ghost buttons use --colour-brand-primary (#5588bb) text on transparent/white background, resulting in approximately 3.9:1 contrast. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 542: `color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Danger buttons use white text (#ffffff) on --colour-error (#ee9999), resulting in approximately 1.6:1 contrast. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text and is nearly invisible to many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 551: `background-color: var(--colour-error-dark); border-color: var(--colour-error-dark);` (and keep white text)
