issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the navigation drawer, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open or closed state. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1654: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` in the open function. Line 1674: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` in the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu control the visibility of dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded="true" or aria-expanded="false" on the trigger buttons. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1748: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` at the start of the _openInstance function. Line 1762: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of the _closeInstance function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the FAQ answer panel and adds CSS classes, but unlike the close function, it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the content has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2648: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: In mobileNav.init, the trigger button is obtained from the DOM, but aria-expanded="false" is not set initially even though the navigation drawer starts in a closed state. Screen readers may not recognize the button as controlling an expandable region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown menu in dropdownMenu.register, the trigger button does not receive an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute even though the panel is hidden by default. This violates the requirement that ARIA states must reflect the current state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1725: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer.mount function creates a listbox for search suggestions and assigns aria-controls, but it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the combobox input initially. Since the suggestion list starts hidden, the input must indicate this state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2110: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and product cards are shown or hidden, the function updates the count badge and empty state visually but does not announce the results to screen reader users via a live region. Users are not informed how many products match the selected filters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2240: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing periods, the prices update visually with animation, but no announcement is made to screen reader users to inform them of the price changes or the newly selected billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2470: After `_syncToggleState(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions accept an announceToSR parameter but do not use it to trigger live region announcements. When FAQ items expand or collapse, screen reader users receive no confirmation of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2648: In open, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` before the emit call. Line 2675: In close, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no announcement is made to screen reader users. This is particularly problematic when the dropdown contains dynamically loaded content or when the user is uncertain whether the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1748: In _openInstance, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after opening the panel. Line 1762: In _closeInstance, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` after closing the panel.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible content significantly, but no announcement is made to inform screen reader users that the navigation menu is now open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1654: In open, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`. Line 1674: In close, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode toggle missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between grid and list view modes for product display, the layout changes visually but no announcement is made to screen reader users to confirm the view mode change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2320: After `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Switched to ${mode} view`);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter sort change missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the product sort order changes (e.g., by price or name), the products reorder visually but no announcement is made to inform screen reader users of the new sort criteria or direction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2290: After `_syncUrl();`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Sorted by ${s.sortKey}, ${s.sortDir === 'asc' ? 'ascending' : 'descending'}`);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter text filter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users type in the text search filter, the product grid updates dynamically after a debounce delay, but no announcement is made to inform screen reader users how many results match their search query.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2305: Inside the _filterByText debounced function, after `applyFilters();`, add `const count = productCardRegistry.getAll().filter(e => e.visible).length; liveRegion.announce(`${count} products match your search`);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap. When the drawer opens, keyboard focus is not trapped within the navigation menu, allowing users to tab out of the modal context and into the underlying page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620: After `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer || SELECTORS.primaryNav);`, add `if (_drawer) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger, escapeDeactivates: true }); }`. Line 1654: In open, replace `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.activate();` with `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open function toggles CSS classes and emits events but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state of the navigation menu to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2035: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer close function removes CSS classes and deactivates the focus trap but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The button continues to report an expanded state after the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2055: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `if (_trap && _trap.isActive()) _trap.deactivate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2185: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu close function removes CSS classes and sets the hidden attribute on the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving assistive technologies unaware the menu closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2200: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open missing aria-expanded update on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion panel open function updates state and shows recent queries but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input element, failing to communicate the expanded state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3055: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_searchState.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _close missing aria-expanded update on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion panel close function hides the listbox but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the combobox input, leaving the input in an expanded state for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3070: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState missing aria-pressed on period buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons are updated with CSS classes indicating active state but do not have aria-pressed set to reflect the pressed state, preventing screen readers from announcing which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3855: Insert `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel togglePlay missing aria-pressed on play button
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause toggle updates the _playing state and aria-label but does not set aria-pressed on the play button, so screen readers cannot distinguish between playing and paused states.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4385: Insert `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing));` after `if (_playBtn) {` block where aria-label is updated

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator _renderResult missing aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the function calls srUtils.markInvalid but does not set the aria-invalid attribute to "true" on the invalid field, so screen readers do not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4560: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` in the else branch (when !result.valid) before or after the srUtils.markInvalid call

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearing on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, leaving the field marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1630: Insert `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after removing the aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialisation sets up event listeners but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the drawer is closed by default on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2090: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;` and the event listener attachments

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu initialisation registers triggers and panels but does not set aria-expanded="false" on trigger buttons when the panels are hidden by default on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2260: Insert `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the register function after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The site search initialisation configures the combobox but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the input element when the suggestion list is closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3110: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the event bindings or after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The filter application updates visible product cards and result counts but does not announce the filter results via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3560: Insert `liveRegion.announce(\`\${totalMatches} products found\`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period change updates price displays across all cards but does not announce the change via a live region, so screen reader users are not informed that prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3880: Insert `liveRegion.announce(\`Billing period changed to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}\`);` after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips render missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are removed or the filter state changes, the render function updates the chip container but does not announce the change via a live region, leaving users unaware that filters have been removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3730: Insert `if (chips.length === 0) { liveRegion.announce('All filters removed'); } else { liveRegion.announce(\`Filter \${chips[chips.length-1].label} removed\`); }` at the end of the render function before the closing brace

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the mobile navigation drawer by removing CSS classes but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element, leaving it in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2055: Insert `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-hidden on panel
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the dropdown panel by setting the hidden attribute after a timeout but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the panel element when it is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2200: Insert `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` inside the setTimeout callback after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestionListRenderer hide missing aria-hidden on listbox
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the suggestion listbox by setting the hidden attribute but does not set aria-hidden="true" on the listbox element.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3025: Insert `setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` after `_listEl.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestionListRenderer show missing aria-hidden removal
issuesFound.explanation: The function shows the suggestion listbox by removing the hidden attribute but does not remove aria-hidden or set it to "false", potentially leaving it hidden from assistive technologies if it was previously set.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open and close functions add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1614: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);
  Line 1634: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); after removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions manipulate panel visibility and CSS classes but fail to synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button, leaving screen reader users unaware of the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1712: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');
  Line 1726: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function sets the open state and animates the panel height but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an asymmetry where the state is only announced when closing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2644: Add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); after addClass(item.el, CSS.open);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input field, so assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine that the field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244: Add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation state, the function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, potentially leaving assistive technologies thinking the field is still invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1255: Add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); or field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); after removing the describedby reference.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is initialised without aria-expanded="false", so screen readers do not know the drawer is collapsed when the page first loads.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1674: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after _initialised = true;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering dropdown triggers, the code ensures aria-haspopup is present but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button for the closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1756: Add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); after setAttr(trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, panelId);

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the visible card count updates in the DOM but the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the filter has been applied or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2224: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterAll.replace('{n}', String(totalMatches))); after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing periods, the price values update visually but the change is not announced via the live region, so screen reader users are not informed of the price change or the new billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2434: Add liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly); after _updateSavingsMessage(period);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding and collapsing FAQ panels changes visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers, leaving users unaware whether the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2644: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question)); in the open function.
  Line 2666: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question)); in the close function.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Activating the scroll-to-top button moves the viewport but does not announce the action to screen readers, potentially causing confusion about what occurred.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1944: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop); at the beginning of the _onClick function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the page layout significantly but does not announce the state change to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1614: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen); in the open function.
  Line 1634: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed); in the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus reveals or hides navigation options without announcing the change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1712: Add liveRegion.announce('Menu opened'); in _openInstance.
  Line 1726: Add liveRegion.announce('Menu closed'); in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, it remains in the accessibility tree because the code only removes CSS classes without setting aria-hidden="true", potentially allowing screen reader users to navigate to invisible content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1634: Add _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); in the close function, and add _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); in the open function (line 1614).

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-selected

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby when in error state, but FormGroup does not render an ErrorMessage with this ID because errorId prop is not passed. The reference points to a non-existent element.

issuesFound.title: Contact form inputs broken aria-describedby references

issuesFound.fix: Line 2014: Pass errorId="contact-name-error" to FormGroup for each field, matching the ID referenced in the input's aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop div uses onClick without keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav backdrop div has an onClick handler to close the menu but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers (Enter/Space). Keyboard-only users cannot dismiss the menu by clicking the backdrop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1189: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler that calls onClose for Enter/Space keys, or remove the onClick and rely on the close button and Escape key.

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div uses onClick without keyboard accessibility
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has an onClick handler to close the dialog but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. While Escape is supported, the click target itself is not accessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2088: Add role="button", tabIndex={0}, and onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space, or remove the onClick from the overlay and restrict closing to the close button and Escape key.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron symbol (▲/▼) inside the dropdown trigger is decorative and duplicates the state information already conveyed by the button's aria-expanded state. Without aria-hidden="true", screen readers may announce the arrow character as "black up-pointing triangle" or similar, creating noise.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1038: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon (☀️/🌙/💻) and no visible text. The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop to provide an accessible name, but none is provided, leaving screen reader users unaware of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1190: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button toggles the answer panel using the isOpen prop. It has aria-controls pointing to the panel but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether the answer is currently displayed or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1741: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-controls={panelId} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple nav elements (DesktopNav, MobileNav, FooterNavGroup x2). The DesktopNav nav has no aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it impossible for screen reader users to distinguish it from other navigation regions when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1070: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Desktop navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel contains a nav element that lacks an accessible name. Since multiple nav elements exist on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this region from the desktop navigation or footer navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1124: <nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing card is rendered as an article element containing a heading with id={headingId}, but the article lacks aria-labelledby. In a grid of pricing cards, screen reader users cannot identify which plan each card represents when navigating by regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1565: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section contains a heading with id="hero-heading" but the section element lacks aria-labelledby. As a major page section, it should be labelled to help screen reader users navigate by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1294: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics bar contains a heading with id="stats-heading" but the section lacks aria-labelledby. This prevents screen reader users from identifying the purpose of this region when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1326: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk (*) marking required fields is not hidden from screen readers. While a VisuallyHidden span provides the text "(required)", the asterisk itself may be announced as "star", creating redundant or confusing output.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~880: *

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when an error exists, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID is rendered in the DOM. This creates a broken ARIA reference that fails WCAG 1.3.1 and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1835: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The name input references 'contact-name-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup receives error={...} instead of errorMessage and lacks errorId, so the error message element is not rendered with the expected ID. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1878: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'contact-email-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, so the error message element is not rendered with the expected ID, creating a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1892: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input references 'contact-subject-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, so the error message element is not rendered with the expected ID, creating a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1906: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea references 'contact-message-error' in aria-describedby, but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, so the error message element is not rendered with the expected ID, creating a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1920: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required hint={`Up to ${MSG_MAX} characters.`} errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it displays a spinner and disables the button, but it does not communicate the loading state to assistive technology via aria-busy. Screen reader users may not understand why the button is unresponsive.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~958: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button contains only the "‹" character and no text content. The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop to provide an accessible name, but none is provided, leaving screen reader users unaware of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1722: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only the "›" character and no text content. The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop to provide an accessible name, but none is provided, leaving screen reader users unaware of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1727: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badges group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The trust badges row contains multiple related items (WCAG badge, SOC 2 badge, GDPR badge, G2 rating) but lacks an accessible name. As a group of related content not covered by a heading, it should have an aria-label to describe the group's purpose to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1315: <div className="hero-section__trust" aria-label="Trust badges and certifications">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection CTAs group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The call-to-action button group contains two related buttons ("Get started for free" and "Book a demo") but lacks an accessible name. As a sub-group of related controls, it should have an aria-label to identify the group to screen reader users navigating by regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1308: <div className="hero-section__ctas" aria-label="Call to action">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links are contained in a nav element that lacks an accessible name. Since multiple nav elements exist on the page (desktop, mobile, footer, social), screen reader users cannot distinguish this utility navigation from the main navigation landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~904: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: StatItem value not using accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The statistic value (e.g., "40,000+") is displayed without using the valueLabel property (e.g., "40,000 plus") provided in the Stat interface. Screen readers may misinterpret the plus sign or other formatting, failing to convey the intended meaning clearly.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1336:

{stat.value}
